note: Both accessibility requirements apply to color use in brand materials.
The approved email scale provides the sizes used to establish the required visual hierarchy.
The campaign-headline exception refines the general digital/email headline typeface hierarchy.
The campaign headline is an explicit ALL-CAPS exception to the general prohibition.
Chart and infographic headlines are an explicit ALL-CAPS exception to the general prohibition.
Both rules govern permissible use of bold emphasis within text.
Both rules restrict non-bold typographic emphasis treatments.
The standalone-section rule specifies how the general first-mention abbreviation pattern must be re-established when sections are assembled independently.
The general low-vision requirement adds email size and light-background stroke guidance to the brand icon system.
Both support the patient-brochure-informed approach to visually engaging, patient-centered section content.
Both govern section-level visual separation and hierarchy.
The email GIF requirements are a specific hard accessibility application of the broader motion guidance.
Code compatibility conventions apply when implementing the modular sections and their declared backgrounds.
